expectations.text: References the feature-dev skill's Phase 6 steps directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Response quotes Phase 6 mandates directly: 'Phase 6 mandates independent code-reviewer agents' and 'Phase 6 calls for multiple agents in parallel covering different focuses — simplicity, correctness, conventions.'

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.
expectations.evidence: Response mentions only code-reviewer agents as Phase 6 requirements. Code-simplifier is never mentioned anywhere in the transcript. The proposal is rejected solely on independence/parallel-coverage grounds, not because code-simplifier is a separate required subagent.

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: 'A single combined agent collapses that to one opinionated pass.' and 'A single combined review-and-simplify agent can't replicate parallel independent perspectives; it will deprioritize coverage in at least one area.'

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: Code-simplifier is never mentioned in the transcript. No ordering statement about code-simplifier is possible.

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.
expectations.evidence: 'Verdict: Not acceptable.' is given at the top, and 'Saving time by merging agents is not an approved optimization' is stated explicitly.
